name: Kingdom
situationOnTree: At the Base of the Pillar of Equilibrium
gods: Sobek, the Egyptian Crocodile God; Persephone; Ceres; Lakshmi; the Sphinx; Shekinah
magicalImage: A young woman in a robe of mixed earth colors, her feet bare and her hair unbound, sitting on a throne of roughly shaped black stone.
symbols: The Altar, the Equal-Armed Cross, the Temple, the Tree of Knowledge of Good and Evil
tarotMinors: The Four Tens; The Four Pages Or Princesses

name: Foundation
situationOnTree: Towards the Base of the Pillar of Equilibrium; Below Tiphareth and Above Malkuth
gods: Shu, Egyptian God of Air, Holding Nuit Heaven Apart From Seb the Earth; Hindu Ganesha, Remover of Obstacles, Who Supports the Universe While Standing on A Tortoise; Diana, Isis, and All Lunar Goddesses; Anima Mundi; Collective Unconscious
magicalImage: A beautiful and very strong naked man standing upon a cube of stone, his arms raised in the position of Atlas holding up the sky.
bodyCorrespondence: Genitals; the Procreative Organ in the Male; the Womb in the Female

name: Splendor
situationOnTree: At the Foot of the Pillar of Severity
gods: Mercury, Hermes, Egyptian Jackal-Headed God Anubis, Hindu Monkey God Hanuman
magicalImage: A winged hermaphrodite, wearing an orange loincloth and sandals, holding a staff in its right hand and a lantern in its left.
bodyCorrespondence: Loins and Legs. Right Hip (Western Tradition). Left Leg (Hebraic Tradition)

name: Victory
situationOnTree: At the Foot of the Pillar of Mercy
gods: Solar victory of dawn over darkness, Nike, Venus, Aphrodite, Hathor, Bhavani
magicalImage: A beautiful naked woman. Her hair is long and unbound, and she wears a crown of deep red roses and green leaves; this is her only ornament.
bodyCorrespondence: Loins, hips, and legs. Left hip (Western tradition). Right leg (Hebraic tradition)
perfumes: Rose, Parsley, Bay Laurel, Red Sandal, Sandalwood, Benzoin

name: Beauty
situationOnTree: In the center of the Pillar of Equilibrium
gods: Ra, Apollo, Hari (Shri Krishna), Adonis, Iacchus, Rama, Asar (all beautiful/handsome gods), Dionysus (Bacchus), Balder (resurrected gods)
magicalImage: A naked child with golden hair, standing with its arms upraised; a king in robes and crown of gold, dark-haired, bearded, seated on a throne, holding a sword in his right hand and a wand topped with a lotus blossom in his left; a naked man crucified on a black leafless tree, tied to the tree at wrists and ankles with bloody ropes.
symbols: The cube, the cross of six squares, the truncated pyramid, the lamen, the rosy cross, the calvary cross, the Vav in the Tetragrammaton, lion, phoenix, pelican
tarotMinors: The Four Sixes; The Four Knights or Princes
perfumes: Acacia, vine, gum of Olibanum perfume

name: Severity
situationOnTree: In the center of the Pillar of Severity
gods: Mars (Ares), Sakti (lower form than Binah), Nephthys, Thor
magicalImage: A warrior queen in full armor, standing in a red chariot pulled by two roan horses, her head bare except for a crown, her dark hair loose, and a drawn sword in her hand.
symbols: The pentagram, the pentagon, the five-petalled tudor rose, the sword, the spear, the scourge, the chain, basilisk
bodyCorrespondence: Right shoulder (Western tradition). Left shoulder (Hebraic tradition)

name: Mercy
situationOnTree: In the center of the Pillar of Mercy
gods: Poseidon, Jupiter (as god of rain, storms and thunder), Zeus armed with thunder and lightning, Indra, Amoun, Thor, Aeger
magicalImage: An old but mighty king sitting on a sapphire throne, robed in blue and violet, a golden crown on his head and a scepter and orb in his hands.
symbols: The solid figure, tetrahedron, pyramid, equal-armed cross, orb, wand, scepter, crook, unicorn, horse
bodyCorrespondence: Left shoulder or left arm (Western tradition). Right shoulder (Hebraic tradition)

name: Understanding
situationOnTree: At the head of the Pillar of Severity in the Supernal Triangle
gods: Kronos, Frigg, Sakti, Maya, Kwan Yin, Yin, Kali, Isis
magicalImage: An old woman with long white hair, wearing a simple black robe, holding a rough wooden staff. She is facing toward the viewer's right.
symbols: All cteic (vaginal) symbols, the vesica piscis, the cup or chalice, the Outer Robe of Concealment, the Heh in the Tetragrammaton, brooding dove
tarotMinors: The Four Threes; The Four Queens
bodyCorrespondence: Right eye or right side of the head (Western tradition). Left eye (Hebraic tradition)

name: Wisdom
situationOnTree: At the head of the Pillar of Mercy in the Supernal Triangle
gods: Tahuti (Thoth), Pallas Athena (Minerva), Maat, Uranus, Hermes, Yang, Kwan Shi Yin, Vishnu, Ishvara
magicalImage: An old man with a long white beard, wearing a plain gray robe and holding a rough wooden staff. He is facing the viewer's left, and looking slightly upwards.
symbols: All phallic symbols, the Yod of Tetragrammaton, the Inner Robe of Glory, the standing stone, the tower, the uplifted rod of power, the straight line.
tarotMinors: The Four Twos; The Four Kings (Knights in Thoth)
bodyCorrespondence: Left eye or left side of the head (Western tradition). Right eye (Hebraic tradition)

name: Crown
situationOnTree: At the head of the Pillar of Equilibrium in the Supernal Triangle
magicalImage: The faint outline of a human face looking toward the viewer's right, seen through brilliant light. No details, not even gender, can be made out.
symbols: The point, the crown, the point within a circle, swan, hawk
microcosm: Yechidah, the spiritual essence of the self
bodyCorrespondence: Above the crown of the head
